description_long: This is a large, double-edged sword with a straight, broad blade that tapers to a pointed tip. The blade is metallic and appears to be forged from steel, with a matte to slightly glossy finish. The crossguard is straight and extends horizontally, ending in rounded tips, providing hand protection and balance. The handle is cylindrical, dark in color, and capped with a rounded pommel, likely made of metal for counterweight. The overall design is reminiscent of a medieval longsword, with minimal ornamentation and a focus on functionality. The sword's imposing size and robust construction suggest it is intended for two-handed use and would be a formidable weapon or display piece in an urban context.
description: A large, double-edged medieval-style sword with a straight blade, horizontal crossguard, and rounded pommel. The design is functional and robust, with a dark handle and metallic blade.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full width of the straight blade, crossguard, and rounded pommel.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the thin profile of the blade and handle, with the crossguard appearing as a narrow line.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, displaying the blade, crossguard, and pommel from the opposite side.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the thinness of the blade and handle.
attribute_handle_design: straight cylindrical handle, dark color, rounded metal pommel, minimal texture
